page: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It features an arm64 architecture powered by an NVIDIA Grace Neoverse V2 processor with 72 physical cores and 72 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.67 GB of RAM per core.

This server integrates a single NVIDIA Hopper GH200 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 960 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is designated as shared, which represents a potential resource-sharing tradeoff despite the bare metal classification of the server.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of the Grace CPU and Hopper GPU on a single module provides high resource density for memory-intensive and accelerated workloads. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as large language model inference, data science jobs, machine learning training, and high-performance computing tasks requiring unified memory architectures.
description: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server in the Bare Metal NVMe family, built on the arm64 architecture. It features an NVIDIA Grace Neoverse V2 processor with 72 physical cores operating at 3.1 GHz, paired with 480.0 GB of system memory. The server includes a bundled NVIDIA Hopper GH200 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Although classified as bare metal, the CPU allocation is designated as shared. This high-density hardware configuration is designed for accelerated workloads, including machine learning training, large language model inference, data science, and high-performance computing applications that benefit from the integrated Grace Hopper architecture.

tagline: An arm64 bare metal server combining a seventy-two core processor with a ninety-six gigabyte GPU for accelerated computing.
